notes: missing owned selection entries ignored without request failure
authorized sender creates exactly one unread notification
created notification must become visible in recipient inbox

bounded_contexts.name: Access Control Context
responsibilities: authorize sender role for notification creation

bounded_contexts.name: Notification Management Context
responsibilities: apply single-notification read transition after successful detail retrieval
delete owned notifications by one, selected-set, and all-owned scopes

aggregates.name: NotificationAggregate

members.name: NotificationDomainService
operations: createUnread(UserId recipientUserId, NotificationType type, NotificationContent content, String? sourceKey)

members.name: NotificationDeletionPolicy
operations: keepExistingOwned(NotificationSelection selection, List<Notification> requestedExistingNotifications, UserId requesterId)

states.name: UNREAD
states.meaning: notification exists and has not yet been successfully retrieved in detail by its recipient

states.name: READ
states.meaning: notification exists and has been successfully retrieved in detail by its recipient

invariants: all query and delete effects remain within authenticated requester Recipient User ID scope
Notification Inbox is a derived recipient-scoped query view, not a separate persisted entity
inbox list is always latest-first and paginated
unread list and unread count never change NotificationReadState
successful detail retrieval changes only the retrieved notification from UNREAD to READ
valid create request persists exactly one unread notification for the specified recipient
create requires Recipient User ID, Notification type, title, and content; targetUrl is optional
single delete and selected-set delete reject the whole request when any foreign-owned notification is included
selected-set delete ignores already-missing owned notifications and deletes only remaining existing owned notifications
selected-set delete still succeeds when zero existing owned notifications remain after filtering
all-owned delete applies only to requester-owned notifications at request time
delete removes the aggregate; no persistent Deleted state is accepted in this ChangeSet

resolution_log.resolution: UC-030, UC-031, and UC-032 share one NotificationAggregate rooted at persisted Notification
Notification Inbox is a recipient-scoped query view, not a separate stored entity
baseline boolean isRead is normalized into shared NotificationReadState value object with UNREAD and READ values
shared contract requires content: String; optional content proposal from UC-030 is not retained
deletion removes the aggregate and does not introduce a persistent Deleted state
authentication and sender-role authorization are normalized as complementary responsibilities inside Access Control Context
sentAt remains part of Notification because latest-first inbox ordering depends on it
sourceKey remains an optional shared identifier
delivery-status semantics are outside the affected use-case evidence and are not added to the canonical aggregate in this stage
